otherwise.choose:
<when condition="@{
                    // Retrieve variables
                    string currentVerStr = (string)context.Variables["currentAppVersion"];
                    string minVerStr = (string)context.Variables["minVerStr"]; 

                    // FIX: Manual semantic version comparison using string split and int.TryParse 
                    // to avoid the "System.Version is not supported" error.
                    
                    string[] vCurrParts = currentVerStr.Split('.');
                    string[] vMinParts = minVerStr.Split('.');

                    int maxLen = Math.Max(vCurrParts.Length, vMinParts.Length);

                    for (int i = 0; i < maxLen; i++) {
                        int curr = 0;
                        int min = 0;
                        
                        // Attempt to parse the current part, default to 0 if missing or invalid
                        if (i < vCurrParts.Length && int.TryParse(vCurrParts[i], out int resultCurr)) {
                            curr = resultCurr;
                        }

                        // Attempt to parse the minimum part, default to 0 if missing or invalid
                        if (i < vMinParts.Length && int.TryParse(vMinParts[i], out int resultMin)) {
                            min = resultMin;
                        }

                        if (curr < min) {
                            return true; // Current is lower
                        }
                        
                        if (curr > min) {
                            return false; // Current is higher or equal
                        }
                    }
                    return false; // Versions are equal (current is not lower)

                }">
						
						
							
							
								application/json
							
							@{
                            string requiredVersion = (string)context.Variables["minVerStr"];
                            string clientType = (string)context.Variables["user-agent"];
                            
                            // Construct the dynamic error message
                            string errorMessage = "We are sorry but in order to continue to use the LifeData app you will need to update to the most recent version for compatibility reasons. Please click “Update” below and you can update the app from the App Store.";

                            // Use verbatim string literal ($@"") for clean, supported JSON construction.
                            // The .Replace is for JSON-safe escaping, even though this message has no quotes.
                            return $@"{{
                                ""ServerResponse"": ""[]"",
                                ""ServerError"": {{
                                    ""ErrorCode"": 50,
                                    ""ErrorTitle"": ""Unsupported Version Detected"",
                                    ""ErrorMessage"": ""{errorMessage}""
                                }}
                            }}";
                        }
